concept: Dance / Move Rhythmically / Express Through Body Movement
description: To move the body in rhythm, whether to music or inner impulse—expressing through gesture what words cannot hold, celebrating through motion, communicating through the oldest language of bodies in space. 'Rotiku' is movement become meaning.
sound_symbolism: The word moves in rhythm. 'Ro' rolls into motion, the dance beginning. Then 'ti' steps with dental precision, feet finding beat. Finally 'ku' grounds with velar embodiment, the whole body engaged in movement.
grammatical_notes: 'mia rotiku' (I dance). Can specify occasion with 'lila': 'mia lila holia rotiku' (I dance for the festival). A dance is the event noun: 'phelora rotiku' (beautiful dance). Related to wile (play) but specifically rhythmic bodily movement.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The dancing body traces the organic curves that define natural beauty—flow made visible, rhythm incarnate.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Dance predates written language—communities danced for harvest, for healing, for rites of passage. Movement carries ancestral memory.
